_comment: Golden queries template for neuralmind benchmark --quality. Copy this to tests/fixtures/benchmark_queries_custom.json and register the suite in evals/quality/runner.py SUITES. See tests/benchmark/golden_queries.yaml for a fully-documented 40-query reference example.

queries.question: Where is getUserById defined?
queries._note: Use shape 'identity' for exact-symbol lookups. BM25 hybrid (v0.38.0+) significantly improves recall on these queries.

queries.question: A user login is failing without any error message — which file contains credential validation?
I need to add an email_verified column to users — which files would change?
I just changed how JWT tokens are signed — what other files should I review?

_usage.step_1: Write 20-30 queries based on real questions your team asks each week. Cover all four categories: architecture, bug-localization, refactor, next-edit.
_usage.step_2: Set expected_modules to file paths only (no line numbers). Two or three files per cross-file query; one for focused/identity queries.
_usage.step_3: Set shape: 'focused' (one expected module), 'cross-file' (2-3 modules), or 'identity' (exact-symbol lookup like a function name).
_usage.step_4: Save as tests/fixtures/benchmark_queries_custom.json, register in evals/quality/runner.py SUITES, then run: neuralmind benchmark --quality
_usage.step_5: For public OSS repos, also run: neuralmind benchmark --public
_usage.step_6: Check autotune is active after 50+ queries: neuralmind self-improve status
_usage.pro_tip: Start with 5 queries around your #1 bug area. If auth bugs happen most, write 5 auth queries — that's where ROI shows fastest. Use shape='identity' for function-name lookups to verify BM25 hybrid is working.
